ProjectHeader.description: Story 0.11 substrate (Phase 3 decision L2). Identifies the target project. Phase 2 single-project deployments accept the header for forward-compatibility: the value must equal the configured project name (case-insensitive after trim) or the reserved sentinel `default`. Mismatching values return 403 `project_not_found`. Absent header is accepted in Phase 2 (with a one-time per-process WARN log) and will become required in Phase 4 multi-project mode.
ProjectHeader.name: X-Anseo-Project

Unauthorized.description: Missing, malformed, or revoked X-Anseo-API-Key. The body shape is intentionally opaque so an unauthenticated caller cannot distinguish 'wrong key' from 'auth backend down'.

target.description: URL, sitemap URL, file:// URL, or local HTML fixture path.

ClickHouseInstallAccepted.description: Story 15.1 — 202 response from POST /v1/setup/clickhouse/install. `install_id` is a ULID the caller can use to subscribe to the SSE progress stream. MOCK in 15.1; real Docker calls land in Story 15.3.

stream.description: Path to the SSE progress stream.

ClickHouseInstallEvent.description: Story 15.1 — one frame of the SSE install stream. Step ordering: docker_detected → image_pulling → container_starting → provisioning_user → applying_migrations → running_parity_test → complete.

ComparisonsResponse.description: Story 0.8 `GET /v1/comparisons` matrix payload — mirrors the MCP CompareBrandsOutput shape (architecture-phase3-mcp-server.md §3.3). Determinism contract: rows ordered (prompt_name ASC, provider ASC); cells ordered [brand, ...competitors_in_caller_order]; absent subjects carry ranking:null (NOT omitted).

ContributionStatus.description: Outcome of the benchmark consent + envelope gate. `sealed`: opted in with a KEK, redacted + sealed. `skipped_not_opted_in`: no active opt-in. `kek_missing`: opted in but no per-project KEK available, so the contribution could NOT be sealed (the run is still recorded; benchmark data is flagged, never silently dropped). `redaction_rejected`: redaction refused the run (e.g. stale consent terms).

reason.description: Present only when status is redaction_rejected.

CrawlReferReport.description: Roadmap Epic 33 crawl-to-refer ratio. When referral attribution is unavailable, state is crawls_only and ratio is null.

lines.description: Raw access-log lines (nginx/Apache), one hit per line.

CrawlerMetricsResponse.description: Roadmap Epic 31 crawler observability metrics. Headline payloads exclude unverified crawler hits unless `include_unverified=true` is requested.

prompt_name.description: Slug-safe prompt identifier declared in anseo.yaml.

GenerateRecommendationsAccepted.description: Story 19.6 — 202 response from POST /v1/recommendations/generate. Per the Phase 2 async-write pattern, `status_url` points at the list endpoint where the results are readable.

IngestRunRequest.description: One externally-executed prompt run submitted for ingestion.

prompt_slug.description: Slug-safe identifier of a prompt already declared in the resolved project.

provider.description: Provider the external run was executed against (e.g. openai).

model.description: Provider model/version string the external run used.

raw_response.description: Canonical external provider payload. May be plain text or structured JSON.

response_text.description: Compatibility field for early clients that only captured plain response text before raw_response became canonical.

citation_domains.description: Optional compatibility/input hint for source domains observed in citations. When omitted, extracted from response_text or raw_response text when possible.

observed_rank.description: The brand's observed rank in this run, if computed by the caller.

observed_at.description: When the external run was observed. Defaults to now.

contribute.description: Opt this run into the anonymous benchmark. Defaults to false. A true value with no per-project KEK is rejected 403 kek_missing. When the project also has an active benchmark opt-in on the current terms, a true run is redacted and envelope-sealed under the project KEK (Story 40.4); otherwise the run is recorded but the contribution is reported as skipped/blocked, never silently dropped.

IngestRunResponse.description: Result of recording an external run, including the benchmark contribution outcome.

OperatorEntity.description: Story 48.4 — an entity (claimed brand) as seen by the operator entity-admin surface.

OperatorVerificationAttempt.description: Story 48.4 — one row of an entity's append-only verification attempt history.

OperatorEntityDetail.description: Story 48.4 — entity detail: the OperatorEntity fields plus its verification_attempts (newest-first).

OperatorConsentRecord.description: Story 49.0 — one row of the OSS-owned benchmark_consent ledger as seen by the Plane-1 operator read.

OperatorConsentEvent.description: Story 49.0 — an opt-in/opt-out event projected from benchmark_consent (event + terms_version + timestamp).

OperatorBenchmarkGate.description: Story 49.0 (D2) — the OSS-owned terms-finalize gate (source of truth; read WITHOUT anseo_admin).

PluginStatus.description: Story 41.2 — runtime activation status of one installed plugin, as resolved at `anseo serve` boot. `anseo plugin list` renders the same fields.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for a `skipped` / `load_error` outcome; absent when `loaded`.

SuitePromptSummary.description: Story 40.5 — one canonical GEO benchmark prompt slug exposed for instrumentation parity helpers.

Recommendation.description: Story 19.6 — a stored GEO Recommendation (architecture-phase3-geo-recommendations.md §8 wire shape) plus its DB lifecycle `state`.

RecommendationListResponse.description: Story 19.6 — cursor-paginated active recommendations. `next_cursor` is null on the last page.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; null when no further pages remain.

SetupStatus.description: Story 15.1 — best-effort status probe across all deployment surfaces. Always returned 200; individual sections carry `state: "unknown"` + an `error` string on failure (per-probe timeout: 1s; 500ms for Docker).

Sm14MetricResponse.description: Story 19.5 — SM-14 adoption metric. `rate` is null when the denominator is zero.

TransitionRecommendationRequest.description: Story 19.6 — lifecycle transition (Story 19.4 state machine). Illegal edges return 409.

info.description: Phase 2 v1 surface — read endpoints, prompt run submission, and the SSE event stream. Auth: X-Anseo-API-Key header carrying an `ogeo_<32 chars>` key. Story 0.11 (Phase 3 substrate) adds the optional X-Anseo-Project header — Phase 2 accepts it for forward compatibility; Phase 4 will require it. Story 15.1 adds three `/v1/setup/*` endpoints (deployment UX substrate).
info.title: Anseo Public REST API

get.summary: Story 47.4 — operator analytics: contribute funnel step counts + per-step drop-off (start → step → complete), verify funnel start/complete/fail counts by method (dns | email) with success rate, and daily badge-embed serves (last 30 d). Read entirely from the aggregate site-event rollups (privacy-safe by construction). Operator-scoped; not gated by X-Anseo-Project. No MCP parity (operator-internal, not agent-facing).

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Trailing window for the contribute + verify funnels. Defaults to 7d; any unsupported value clamps to 7d. Badge embeds are always last 30 d.

get.summary: Story 47.4 — operator analytics: public-site overview from the aggregate rollups — unique sessions per day, top-5 pages, and top-5 referrer domains over the selected window. Privacy-safe (no raw per-visitor rows). Operator-scoped; not gated by X-Anseo-Project. No MCP parity (operator-internal, not agent-facing).

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Trailing window. Defaults to 7d; any unsupported value clamps to 7d.

post.summary: Roadmap Epic 32 — crawl a URL/sitemap and score citation-readiness against in-tree heuristics (Identity, Extractability, Corroboration). Optional CI-gate thresholds.

502.description: Audit crawl failed for the requested target.

get.summary: Roadmap Epic 32 — persisted site-audit history for the project, newest first.

schema.description: Comma-separated; 2..=6 entries. First entry is the subject brand; remainder are competitors in caller-declared order.
Comma-separated prompt names; default = all prompts for the project.
Comma-separated provider names; default = all providers.

get.summary: Phase 3 Story 0.8 — deterministic brand-vs-competitors comparison matrix (substrate for MCP `compare_brands`).

post.summary: Roadmap Epic 31 — ingest raw web-server access-log lines, normalize + verify bot identity, and write crawler events for this project.

get.summary: Roadmap Epic 31 — crawler frequency, top paths, stuck/error pages, and trend. Verified-only by default.
Roadmap Epic 33 — crawl-to-refer ratio by verified bot, degraded to crawls_only until referral attribution exists.

post.summary: Roadmap Epic 31 — Grafana JSON datasource query for crawler trends.

get.summary: Health probe

post.summary: Record an externally-executed prompt run, feeding the same extraction -> redaction -> envelope-sealed-contribution path as native runs.
post.description: Records a prompt run executed against a provider OUTSIDE Anseo's own orchestrator (e.g. via an SDK). The run is persisted as a prompt_run for the project resolved from the X-Anseo-Project header and returns 202 with the new run_id. `raw_response` is the canonical payload field; `response_text` remains accepted as a backward-compatibility convenience for early clients. The optional `contribute` flag (default false) opts this run into the anonymous benchmark: a `contribute: true` request with no per-project KEK is rejected 403 kek_missing; `contribute: false` proceeds regardless of KEK state. A run is redacted (same compile-time-safe Redactor as native runs) and envelope-sealed under the project KEK only when it set `contribute: true` AND the project has an active benchmark opt-in on the current terms; benchmark data is never silently dropped (Story 40.4).

202.description: Run recorded. The contribution field reports whether a benchmark contribution was sealed, skipped, or blocked.

400.description: Malformed request body (e.g. non-slug prompt_slug or empty model).

403.description: contribute=true but the project has no per-project benchmark KEK (kek_missing).

404.description: No project resolved for this request (project_not_found).

422.description: The prompt slug is not declared in the project (prompt_not_found) or the provider does not resolve (provider_not_supported).

429.description: Per-process per-project ingest rate limit exceeded (rate_limited).

get.summary: Story 41.2 — list installed plugins with their runtime activation status (loaded | skipped | load_error), as resolved at serve boot. Operator-scoped; not gated by X-Anseo-Project.

Story 40.5 — list the canonical GEO benchmark prompt slugs (`slug`, `version`, `description`) external instrumentation should reuse for comparable contribution cohorts. Operator-scoped global metadata; not gated by X-Anseo-Project.

Story 41.3 — live plugin registry catalog merged with installed state (drives the dashboard /marketplace page). Degrades to an empty list when the registry is offline so the UI renders its zero-state. Operator-scoped; not gated by X-Anseo-Project.

Story 48.4 — operator entity-admin: list/search claimed brands. Filters claim_status, verification_method, and a case-insensitive domain substring (filters AND together); limit/offset pagination (default limit 50, max 200). Operator-scoped: gated by the global ANSEO_OPERATOR_API_KEY (X-Anseo-API-Key); tenant project keys are rejected with 403. Reached server-to-server by the anseo-web BFF.

200.description: OK — { entities: OperatorEntity[], limit, offset, count }

403.description: Forbidden — a non-operator (e.g. tenant project) key.

get.summary: Story 48.4 — operator entity-admin: one entity by domain plus its full verification_attempts history (newest-first). Operator-scoped (ANSEO_OPERATOR_API_KEY); tenant keys 403.

403.description: Forbidden — a non-operator (e.g. tenant project) key.

post.summary: Story 48.4 — revoke a claimed entity via the SHARED revoke path (set claim_status=revoked + start 14-day grace period + append revocation ledger row) — the same path the daily re-verify job uses, not a fork. Actor read from X-Anseo-Operator-Actor / operator body. Operator-scoped; tenant keys 403.

403.description: Forbidden — a non-operator (e.g. tenant project) key.

post.summary: Story 48.4 — manually mark an entity verified with a REQUIRED recorded reason; verification_method is set to manual_override (distinct from the self-service dns_txt / email_magic_link methods). Empty reason → 400. Operator-scoped; tenant keys 403.

403.description: Forbidden — a non-operator (e.g. tenant project) key.

post.summary: Story 48.4 — re-issue the 43.2 email magic-link verification (mint a fresh challenge + re-send via the comms SMTP wire). Honest failure: if mail is not configured the call returns 502 (no silent success). Operator-scoped; tenant keys 403.

403.description: Forbidden — a non-operator (e.g. tenant project) key.

502.description: Challenge created but the magic-link email could not be sent (SMTP not configured / dispatch failed).

post.summary: Story 48.4 — GDPR erase (two-step, irreversible). A call with no confirm_token returns a short-lived (~5 min) signed HMAC confirm token bound to (domain, actor) and erases NOTHING; a call presenting the matching token transactionally deletes the entity + its verification_attempts + identifiable dispute rows. KEK SAFETY: ProjectKek::destroy (crypto-shred) runs ONLY when the domain maps to EXACTLY ONE project via the identified-contribution linkage; otherwise the response carries kek_destroyed:false with a kek_skip_reason (we never destroy a KEK that could shred unrelated contributors). Operator-scoped; tenant keys 403.

200.description: Either an EraseChallenge (token-less call: { confirm_required, confirm_token, expires_in_secs }) or an EraseResult (confirmed: { erased, entity_rows, attempt_rows, dispute_rows, kek_destroyed, kek_skip_reason? }).

401.description: Missing operator key, or the confirm_token is invalid/expired/not bound to this (domain, operator).

403.description: Forbidden — a non-operator (e.g. tenant project) key.

200.description: OK — { records: OperatorConsentRecord[], limit, offset, count }

get.summary: Story 49.0 (D1) — Plane-1 OSS operator read of the OSS-owned benchmark_consent ledger. Filters: tier (anonymous|brand_visibility), project (id), event (optin|optout), from/to (RFC3339); limit/offset pagination (default 50, max 200). Read-only. Operator-scoped (ANSEO_OPERATOR_API_KEY); tenant keys 403. benchmark-service untouched.

200.description: OK — { events: OperatorConsentEvent[], limit, offset, count }

get.summary: Story 49.0 (D1) — opt-in/opt-out event stream projected from the OSS-owned benchmark_consent ledger (event + terms_version + timestamp). Same filters/pagination as consent/records. Read-only. Operator-scoped; tenant keys 403.

Story 49.0 (D1) — per-project crypto-shred/KEK status (active|shredded|pending) derived from non-secret signals only (KEK presence in the secret store + OSS-owned identified-contribution count). NEVER returns key material. Read-only. Operator-scoped; tenant keys 403.

200.description: OK — { density_floor, window_days, segments: { provider, category, window_days, contributor_count, meets_floor }[], count }

get.summary: Story 49.0 (D1) — contribution density per (provider × category × window) feeding the k>=N floor. The density_floor comes from the OSS-owned gate config; meets_floor uses the SAME contributor_count >= floor predicate as the public-benchmark density-floor source of truth (density_check). Read-only. Operator-scoped; tenant keys 403.

200.description: OK — { window_hours, verified, failed, revoked, expired, pending, total }

get.summary: Story 49.0 (D1) — recent verification completions/failures over the 48.4 verification_attempts substrate (counts by terminal status over a look-back window). Read-only. Operator-scoped; tenant keys 403.

Story 49.0 (D2) — read the OSS-owned terms-finalize gate (terms_finalized toggle + active terms_version + density_floor). This is the source of truth: an OSS consumer (CLI optin / ingest) reads it WITHOUT reading anseo_admin (ADR-007). Operator-scoped; tenant keys 403.

put.summary: Story 49.0 (D2) — operator-admin write of the terms-finalize gate. The source of truth lives in OSS (crates/storage), not anseo_admin; a subsequent GET reflects the write. Operator-scoped; tenant keys 403.

post.summary: Story 41.3 — verify (checksum + Ed25519 signature) and record a plugin install from the live registry by id. Operator-scoped; not gated by X-Anseo-Project.

200.description: Install outcome (ok + signature_status + audit_event_id, or a structured error_kind).

404.description: Plugin id not present in the registry index.

delete.summary: Story 41.3 — soft-remove an installed plugin (worker unloads on next restart). Operator-scoped; not gated by X-Anseo-Project.

404.description: No active install for this id.

post.summary: Story 41.3 — re-install the registry-current version of a plugin (no semver resolution; the registry declares the current version). Operator-scoped; not gated by X-Anseo-Project.

404.description: Plugin id not present in the registry index.

get.summary: List active (non-archived) projects. Operator-scoped; not gated by X-Anseo-Project.

post.summary: Create a project from a brand. project_id is derived from the name and returned.

409.description: A project with this derived id already exists.

get.summary: Fetch a single project by id.

post.summary: Soft-delete a project (idempotent). It drops out of the list but its data is preserved.

403.description: Cross-project subscription attempt; key authorized for a different project_id.

get.summary: Server-Sent Events stream of ARCH-17 lifecycle events for one project.

post.summary: Dispatch a one-shot prompt run for an already-declared Prompt and Provider.

schema.description: Opaque page cursor from a previous response's next_cursor.

get.summary: Story 19.6 — cursor-paginated active recommendations for the project, newest first.

503.description: No anseo.yaml loaded; cannot assemble recommendation inputs.

post.summary: Story 19.6 — assemble an EngineInput from the project's live prompts/runs/citations, run the in-process engine, and persist the result. Returns 202 + a status_url per the Phase 2 async-write pattern.

get.summary: Story 19.5+ — per-kind adoption: surfaced / acted / dismissed counts (the what-works-vs-what-doesn't loop). First-party only.
Story 19.5 — SM-14 adoption metric for the project (Acted∨Measured / Surfaced∨later, first-party Kinds only).
Story 19.6 — one recommendation + full traceability. 404 when the row is absent or owned by another project.

400.description: Unknown target state or malformed id.

patch.summary: Story 19.6 — apply a lifecycle transition (Story 19.4 state machine). Illegal transitions return 409.

get.summary: List recent Prompt Runs
Read the DB-authoritative brand config (name, variants, competitors, site_url).

put.summary: Update brand config. Changing the name re-derives project_id (allowed only before the first run) and sets restart_required.

post.summary: Story 15.1 — kick off (MOCK) ClickHouse local-install state machine. Returns 202 with a ULID and an SSE stream URL. Real Docker calls land in Story 15.3.

200.description: text/event-stream — each frame is a ClickHouseInstallEvent serialised as the SSE `data:` field.

get.summary: Story 15.1 — SSE stream of install progress events keyed by `id` (the ULID returned from POST /v1/setup/clickhouse/install). Closes when state reaches `complete` or `failed`.

Story 15.1 — synchronous status probe across Postgres, ClickHouse, worker, webhook target, API keys, and Docker. Always returns 200; individual sections report `state: "unknown"` on probe failure or timeout.
Roadmap Epic 30 — sentiment aggregate under the existing visibility resource.

event_type.description: Must be one of the 10-event taxonomy: page_view, leaderboard_view, brand_profile_view, contribute_start, contribute_step, contribute_complete, verify_start, verify_complete, verify_fail, badge_embed_view. Unknown values are silently dropped (204).

referrer.description: Referrer domain only (never a full URL).

session_id.description: Ephemeral per-visit UUID generated client-side; not linked to identity.

204.description: Accepted, or silently dropped (unknown event_type). No body either way.

429.description: Per-IP rate limit (60/min) exceeded. The IP is not written to any DB table.

post.summary: Story 47.1 — public, unauthenticated privacy-safe site-event ingest. No API key required (the public site posts directly). No PII stored: no IP column, no user IDs; session_id is an ephemeral per-visit UUID. Unknown event_type values are silently dropped with 204 to prevent allowlist enumeration. Rate-limited 60/min per IP at the edge (IP never persisted).
